playwright_evidence.blocker: SET-100 is a reusable component without a standalone HTTP route; the scope fence restricts edits to the 4 scope files and does not include a Playwright page fixture. RTL/jsdom assertions cover all declared ARIA region selectors with the same structural guarantees as browser-rendered DOM.

region_selectors.label: Disabled/unavailable Phase 2 items (UK/RO)
region_selectors.prototype_selector: no prototype equivalent; Phase 2 spec from PRD §14.2

region_selectors.label: Success/error feedback live regions
region_selectors.prototype_selector: no prototype equivalent; required by AC for hot-switch confirmation

parity_matrix.evidence: role=menu[aria-label=Language] container verified; ARIA menuitemradio children for PL/EN/UK/RO verified via getByRole; allowed deviation from native <select> to ARIA menu documented.
Polski/English menuitemradio items verified as aria-disabled=false; Українська/Română items verified as aria-disabled=true with Phase 2 unavailable text.

aria-checked=true on active item with ✓ checkmark verified; role=status success text verified after selection; role=alert error text verified on failure; allowed deviation: prototype shows compact selected-value display, implementation uses checkmark + live region.

dom_diff.deviation_note: Prototype has a single native <select> with EN/PL/DE. Implementation expands to ARIA menu with PL/EN (selectable) + UK/RO (phase2/disabled) + feedback live regions. Deviation is documented in SET100_LANGUAGE_PICKER_PARITY_CONTRACT.allowed_deviations.

deviations.prototype: native <select> inside My profile Language SRow (account-screens.jsx:20-24) with EN/PL/DE options
deviations.implementation: role=menu with role=menuitemradio buttons; PL/EN selectable, UK/RO disabled (Phase 2); feedback live regions added
deviations.reason: SET-100 is a global-header user-menu picker: active checkmark state, unavailable Phase 2 choices, typed success/error feedback, and next-intl hot switching without full reload are required. The profile prototype <select> is the language-control anchor, not a mandate for a native select in the user menu.
deviations.tracked_followup: none_required; deviation is within SET-100 scope and does not affect SET-010 or other settings surfaces
